sanskrit: न तद्भासयते सूर्यो न शशाङ्को न पावकः । यद्गत्वा न निवर्तन्ते तद्धाम परमं मम ॥
hindiTranslation: उसपरमपद को न सूर्य, न चन्द्र और न अग्नि ही प्रकाशित कर सकती है और जिसको प्राप्त होकर जीव लौटकर संसारमें नहीं आते, वही मेरा परमधाम है ॥
englishTranslation: The sun, moon, and fire do not illumine that place. Having reached it, one does not return. That supreme abode is Mine.
essence: The highest home is beyond every ordinary light and beyond return.
speaker: Krishna
simpleMeaning: Krishna says his highest abode cannot be illuminated by ordinary light, and anyone who reaches it does not come back to the cycle of return.
simpleInsight: There is relief in knowing the highest goal is beyond ordinary measures and beyond coming back to the same struggle.
detailedMeaning: Arjuna stands shaken on the battlefield, and Krishna has been describing the highest reality beyond decay and return. Now Krishna names that goal directly: a supreme abode beyond sun, moon, and fire, reached by those who will not fall back into the cycle.
modernRelevance: You leave a hospital room after a final goodbye, and the fluorescent lights feel weak against what just changed. Some thresholds are not about more effort — they are about crossing into a life that cannot be undone.
translationLiteral: न (not) / तत् (that) / भासयते (illumines) / सूर्यः (the sun) / न (not) / शशाङ्कः (the moon) / न (not) / पावकः (fire) / यत् (which) / गत्वा (having gone) / न (not) / निवर्तन्ते (they return) / तत् (that) / धाम (abode) / परमम् (supreme) / मम (Mine)
insight: It shifts attention from temporary sources of guidance to the unchanging goal that ends return.

quoteVariants.text: No light reaches that abode. Once reached, return ends.
What you seek outlasts every light you know.
